description: Final optimized workflow IDs for GatherSampleEvidence. Based on empirical testing in ap-southeast-1.

collect_counts.notes: Sequential scanner - FUSE is fine, pre-localize doesn't help

collect_sv_evidence.notes: Sequential scanner - FUSE is fine, pre-localize causes disk space failure

manta.notes: Random access tool - pre-localize gives 4x speedup (306 -> 77 min). Uses /usr/local/bin/manta/bin/configManta.py

scramble.notes: STATIC storage required for CRAM pre-localization. 12 parallel cluster_identifier -r processes with wait -n concurrency limiter. Max tested: 12 works, 24 fails.

wham_parallel.notes: Single instance, pre-localize once, 24 parallel whamg processes. Avoids 24 redundant CRAM copies.

lessons_learned.pre_localization: Only helps random-access tools (Manta, Wham, Scramble). Sequential scanners (CollectCounts, CollectSVEvidence) are fine on FUSE.
lessons_learned.disk_space: HealthOmics DYNAMIC storage /tmp has limited space. Copying 14.7 GB CRAM to /tmp fails even with 32 GB RAM. Use pre-localize only when the tool needs random access.
lessons_learned.crai_colocation: Manta and Scramble require index co-located with CRAM. Use 'ln -sf' or 'cp' to place .crai next to .cram
lessons_learned.wham_sharding: Per-chromosome sharding with separate runs is wasteful (each copies full CRAM). Single large instance with parallel processes is better.
